scenario: A Vertex banking architect compares the Camunda 8.8 COLLECT and RULE ORDER hit policies for a team running 30000 transactions per day. The architect contrasts their two list-style results and asks what differentiates COLLECT without an aggregator from RULE ORDER in terms of result ordering.
question: What distinguishes COLLECT without an aggregator from RULE ORDER in the Camunda 8 DMN docs?

options.text: RULE ORDER returns one aggregated entry while COLLECT without an aggregator returns the outputs of all satisfied rules as a list in arbitrary order at runtime
RULE ORDER preserves the document order of rules while COLLECT without an aggregator returns the outputs of all satisfied rules as a list in arbitrary order
COLLECT without an aggregator and RULE ORDER both return one entry, but COLLECT picks the first satisfied rule while RULE ORDER picks the highest priority rule
COLLECT without an aggregator and RULE ORDER both return list results in arbitrary order, but only RULE ORDER supports the aggregation attribute at runtime

a.text: Incorrect. RULE ORDER returns a list ordered by rule order rather than a single aggregated entry; aggregation is documented only for COLLECT with the aggregation attribute on the decisionTable element.

b.text: Correct. The docs state RULE ORDER returns the outputs in the order of the rules, while COLLECT without an aggregator returns the outputs of all satisfied rules as a list in arbitrary order with no ordering.

c.text: Incorrect. Neither policy returns a single entry by default; both return list results, and only when COLLECT is paired with an aggregator does the result collapse to a single output entry.

d.text: Incorrect. RULE ORDER does not return an arbitrary-order list; the docs state RULE ORDER preserves the order of the rules, and only COLLECT supports the aggregation attribute on the decisionTable.

explanation: RULE ORDER and COLLECT without an aggregator both return list results, but the docs differentiate them by ordering. RULE ORDER preserves the order of rules in the decision table, whereas COLLECT without an aggregator returns the outputs in an arbitrary order. Only COLLECT supports the aggregation attribute.
